MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Joan Herrington, Esq. Bay Area Employment Law Office (510) 387-5626 jh@baelo.com Attorneys for Plaintiff Rick LAM, (via Zoom)
AMY D. SUPER, Esq., Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-3931 amy.super@sfcityatty.org for Defendant.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as modified.

PLAINTIFF RICK LAM MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Plaintiff's motion to summarily adjudicate the City and County of San Francisco's third, fourth, fifth and twelfth affirmative defenses is granted.

The City "withdraws its third, fourth and twelfth affirmative defenses." (Opp. 2:11.) As to the fifth defense (failure to mitigate), the City says it "has retained a vocational expert to testify." (Id. at 3:22-23.) However, the City's opposition adduces no evidence, and it had the burden of proof. (Mize-Kurzman v. Marin Community College Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 832, 871.) "Failure to mitigate" remains a trial issue, as it is not an affirmative defense.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
